calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions And Attorney?S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jan-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for Joe D. Jones III, Petitioner.

the court confirms with Ms. Wu that opposing counsel is aware of the hearing on this motion. Ms. Wu confirms that opposing counsel advised her via email on Oct-26-2022 that he did not intend to oppose the motion. The court and Ms. Wu disuss and confirm what orders she is seeking via the motion.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for and with Joe Jones, Petitioner; Alec Adams, via Zoom, for Shray Richardson, Trustee.

Ms. Wu confirms that her client has not received any money pursuant to the arbitration award and as discussed at the previous hearing in this matter. Mr. Adams advises that his client has withheld distribution because of a possible capital gains tax payment for which she does not want to be solely responsible. Both Ms. Wu and Mr. Adams offer testimony regarding the arbitration award with Ms. Wu advising that the potential tax issue should have been clarified prior to the arbitration. Mr. Adams reports that the tax issue came to light in April of this year which was after the arbitration which occurred in January of this year.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-31-2022 Continued To Sep-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for Joe D. Jones III, Petitioner; Alec Adams, via Zoom, for Shray Richardson, Respondent.

The court confirms with Ms. Wu that her client has not received any money. Mr. Adams reports that his client is due about $12,000 for payment of home insurance that was not included in the arbitration award. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. Ms. Wu advises that Mr. Adams did not meet his obligation to timely file an opposition to the Arbitration Award. Mr. Adams reports of issues with Ms. Wu's notice regarding this hearing. Ms. Wu offers clarification regarding the notice issue. The court advises that it will be continuing this matter to allow Ms. Wu to properly serve Mr. Adams, pursuant to code. Mr. Adams advises that his client is ready to pay Mr. Jones $100,000 in the interim.

The matter is continued to Sep-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award

Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Continued To Dec-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via ZOOM, for Joe Jones, III, Petitioner.

Ms. Wu reports that the parties have stipulated to binding arbitration and are coordinating with the arbitrator to schedule the arbitration and plans to withdraw the petition once the hearing is scheduled.

The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc., is continued to Dec-06-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee, Appoint A Receiver Or Temporary Trustee, Account, Compel Distribution, And Surcharge Trustee For Harm To The Trust; And Complaint For Declaritory Relief
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Continued To Apr-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee, Appoint A Receiver Or Temporary Trustee, Account, Compel Distribution, And Surcharge Trustee For Harm To The Trust; And Complaint For Declaritory Relief
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Continued To Mar-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Sil Vossler appeared by zoom for and with Joe Jones III by zoom.
Alec Adams appeared by telephone for Shray Richardson (Respondent).

The Court noted that there is a notice issue and asked Mr. Adams if he waives direct notice to his Client. Mr. Adams stated that he does not waive notice on behalf of his Client and that Ms. Richardson needs to be personally served. Mr. Vossler stated that it was an administrative error, that he encouraged Mr. Adams in the interest of judicial economy and to resolve this matter to accept service and waive this notice, and asked if Mr. Adams filed his opposition yesterday. Mr. Adams stated that he did not file an opposition, that his Client intends to make a partial distribution this week, that he will file an opposition once his Client is served and that his hope is to resolve this matter with mediation with retired Judge David Garcia of JAMS. Mr. Adams also stated that they had mediation with Judge Garcia, that they were able to sell the house with Judge Garcia's assistance and that Judge Garcia asked them to come back to him so that he could assist with how to determine who will get what portion from the sale. In addition, Mr. Adams stated that the proceeds are sitting in an account in New York and that he hopes to resolve this outside of the Court's jurisdiction. Mr. Vossler stated that this case is beyond the point where mediation would help, that they already went to mediation, that the Respondent would not agree to anything unless it involved a settlement, that the Respondent sat on this trust for years and that they need Court intervention. Mr. Vossler also stated that the amount that is to be distributed is because they had a contract, that it was not a settlement, that his Client had to get out of the property during the pandemic, that the Respondent keeps saying the money is coming, but it hasn't and that he will serve notice today. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. is continued to March 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Vossler informed the Court that Attorney Kevin Buggy of Open Door Legal will be stepping in.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Order The Immediate Sale Of The Real Property Of The Trust; Empower Co-Trustee And Co-Be
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that this petition has been on calendar several times, that at the last calling of the matter there was some confusion as to who was Counsel, that it required the parties to file a substitution of attorney and that no substitution has been filed. The Petition to Order the Immediate Sale of the Real Property of the Trust is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Order The Immediate Sale Of The Real Property Of The Trust; Empower Co-Trustee And Co-Beneficiary Joe Dudley Jones, Iii To Have The Sole
Off Calendar Aug-25-2020 Continued To Sep-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sil Liapis, Esq., counsel for Joe Dudley Jones III, Co-Trustee (appearing by video conference) pending filing of a Substitution of Attorney; Alec Adams, Esq., counsel for Shray Richardson, Co-Trustee (appearing by video conference).

Mr. Liapis advises the Court of the circumstances occasioning the delay in filing a Substitution of Attorneys and that upon the filing of the same it is his client's intention to dismiss the Petition to sell the Ankeny Street property. The Court acknowledges the intention but in the absence of a filed Substitution of Attorneys form, the Court cannot entertain the proposition at this time. Mr. Adams confirms that the dismissal of the Petition would be agreeable to his client.

The Court continues the matter to September 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. If the substitution and dismissal are filed by that date, no appearance will be required and the Court will take the Petition off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
